finding: `node atm.mjs git commit --auto-stage --json` can exceed the caller timeout after the underlying git commit already succeeds, leaving the operator with a timeout error, a moved `HEAD`, and staged/unstaged residue to reconcile manually.
expectedBehavior: The wrapper should emit phase progress, bound long hook/validator phases, and make successful commit completion observable before any post-commit work can overrun the caller timeout. A retry/status command should identify whether the commit already landed.
evidenceOrRepro: During `TASK-AAO-0151`, the governed commit command timed out after 184 seconds, but `git log -1` showed commit `7eb64669 fix(next): admit unconfirmed broker overlaps` had landed. Residual working-tree changes then had to be inspected and reconciled by diffing staged vs unstaged state. Reconfirmed on 2026-07-09 when wrapper failures returned empty stdout/stderr and required a manual hook pass plus native copyable commit to prove the repo state.
followUp: Keep `tests/cli/git-commit-timeout-and-status.test.ts` coverage that timed-out attempts record HEAD before/after, status/retry commands, and copyable host-git diagnostics; keep using `git commit-status` before any retry after timeout.
